title: execution-event-v1
description: Output contract, executor -> platform: one line of events.jsonl. DERIVED from internal/events.Event, not designed anew — the field shapes are whatever the Go writer actually marshals. Additional top-level properties are tolerated so the executor can add purely additive fields without breaking an older platform; an incompatible change requires a new format_version. Tolerating extra keys is not a leak channel: the semantic validators scan every document recursively and reject any sensitive key (token, secret, pass, key, auth, cred, cookie, session, bearer — case-insensitive substring) whose value is neither null, nor "", nor "<redacted>".

format_version.description: Document format version. Absent, zero, and future versions are rejected. This is the DOCUMENT version, not the executor build version.

run_id.description: Run identifier; same rules as events.ValidateRunID.

ts.description: RFC3339 / RFC3339Nano with a timezone. Go emits nanosecond precision.

phase.description: Phase enum, plus the empty string. Run-level events (run_started, run_completed, run_failed) carry no phase, and Go's `phase` field has no omitempty, so the writer emits "". That is valid, and the schema must say so.

source.title: host reference
source.description: events.HostRef. A non-pointer Go struct, so `omitempty` never fires: the key is always present in the JSON, and both members are always present, possibly as "". Never carries a password, a key, or a port.

destination.title: host reference
destination.description: events.HostRef. A non-pointer Go struct, so `omitempty` never fires: the key is always present in the JSON, and both members are always present, possibly as "". Never carries a password, a key, or a port.

data.description: Free-form payload, extensible. Routed through the redaction net by events.Writer before it is written.
